preview.summary: Group H opener pitting Marcelo Bielsa's organized, midfield-heavy Uruguay against an underdog Saudi Arabia leaning on counters and set pieces. With Spain favored to top the group, both sides treat this as a must-win for second place. Expect a controlled, low-scoring afternoon in the Miami heat.
preview.homeForm: Saudi Arabia arrive ranked around 61st in the world and qualified through Asia as clear underdogs in the group.
preview.awayForm: Uruguay sit 17th in the FIFA rankings and are tipped as the most likely side to join Spain in the knockouts.
preview.homeKey: Salem Al-Dawsari, creative spark and one-on-one threat on the wing.
preview.awayKey: Federico Valverde, box-to-box engine driving Uruguay's midfield.
storylines: Bielsa's structured Uruguay chase a fast start in Group H
Al-Dawsari carries Saudi Arabia's hopes against a deeper squad
Darwin Nunez leads the line for a two-time world champion
preview.h2h: The sides have rarely met competitively, with no prior World Cup meeting between them.
